title: SAN JOSE, CA – Senator Dave Cortese (D-San Jose) issued the following statement regarding his Senate Bill 636 (SB 636) advancing to the Governor’s desk for signing and adoption.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 636 will safeguard the due process rights of workers injured on the job.",
    "If an insurance company harms a worker by modifying or denying their care, this bill ensures that the reviewer responsible can be held accountable to the state’s licensure requirements.",
    "The bill will give California workers access to the medical treatment they deserve.",
    "SB 636 would ensure that the Medical Board of California can discipline medical professionals performing utilization review if they violate practice standards.",
    "Utilization review is the process by which an insurance company uses a medical professional to review then approve, modify, or deny treatment recommendations."
  ]
}
```
